description: Base slurry recipes. One record per base. Read by glazes.json records via base_id field.

bases.name: Standard Clear Gloss Base
bases.water_target: ~3440g added gradually while checking SG. Final SG 1.35 is the guide -- not the water amount.
bases.chemistry_notes: Moderate boron from Frit 3134, calcium from Wollastonite, nepheline provides fluxing alkalis. Well-melted, fluid at cone 6. Good for transparency and edge break.
bases.f_factor_note: Measure at actual SG 1.35 -- never assume. Changes with SG.
bases.notes: Current batch G2926B-001 mixed 2026-06-24. Stock SG 1.39 -- diluting at colorant mixing stage.

bases.name: Chrome-Tin Base
bases.use_for: Chrome-tin colorants ONLY -- Mason stains 6000-6006, raw chrome oxide + tin oxide combinations
bases.water_target: ~3440g added gradually while checking SG. Final SG 1.35 is the guide.
bases.chemistry_notes: High CaO (14.2%), zero MgO, zero ZnO. Boron at acceptable limit for chrome-tin -- monitor. If color fires grey/washed out, boron is the likely cause. Lower by adding whiting or reducing Frit 3134.
bases.f_factor_note: Measure at actual SG 1.35 -- never assume.
bases.why_separate_base: Chrome oxide volatilizes in kiln and contaminates tin-bearing glazes. Chrome-tin colorants require high CaO and low boron to develop pink/red tones rather than grey.
bases.status_note: First test fire June 2026 -- results pending.
